page: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 32 dedicated vCPUs mapped to 16 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor operating at 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes 256.0 GB of system memory, yielding an 8.0 GB memory-to-vCPU ratio. Storage resources consist of 1890 GB of local disk capacity, and the instance is Premium Storage capable. No GPUs or complimentary public IPv4 addresses are included.

The underlying Intel Xeon processor features a 480 MB L3 cache, 2 MB L2 cache, and 48 KB L1d cache. It supports advanced instruction sets including AVX-512 and Advanced Matrix Extensions. In performance testing, the CPU achieved a PassMark CPU score of 48743.5, placing it in the strong tier. Single-threaded gzip compression reached a top-tier result of 36.05 GB/sec, while multi-threaded bzip3 decompression registered a strong 180.33 GB/sec. Stress-ng single-core and multi-core benchmarks returned average-tier results of 2238.28 and 34203.68, respectively.

Memory bandwidth testing showed 2417.14 GB/sec for cached small-block reads, and uncached large-block reads and writes reached 146.04 GB/sec and 62.52 GB/sec, respectively. Database and transactional performance benchmarks returned average-tier results, with PassMark database operations at 14087.31 and Redis SET operations processing 53.04 million operations per second. For machine learning tasks, LLM inference prompt processing with a medium 7B model performed in the strong tier at 190.8 tokens/sec, though text generation on a large 70B model fell into the weak tier at 2.69 tokens/sec.

Given the high memory density of 8 GB per vCPU and the inclusion of 1890 GB of local storage, this instance provides a resource-dense configuration for memory-bound workloads without requiring separate storage attachments. The hardware profile is suited for memory-intensive applications, relational databases, caching layers like Redis, and medium-scale language model inference tasks that leverage Intel AMX instructions.
description: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v7 is an Edsv7-family virtual machine configured with 32 dedicated vCPUs and 256.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor, this instance features 16 physical cores, a 480 MB L3 cache, and 1890 GB of local storage. It supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel AMX. Benchmark results show strong performance in multi-threaded bzip3 decompression and PassMark CPU tests, alongside top-tier single-threaded gzip compression. Memory bandwidth reaches 146.04 GB/sec for uncached large-block reads. With an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio and built-in local storage, this instance is configured for memory-intensive workloads, database operations, caching tiers, and medium-scale LLM inference.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v7 is an Edsv7 virtual machine featuring 32 dedicated Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1890 GB of local storage, designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E32ds_v7 is an Edsv7 VM with 32 Intel Xeon vCPUs, 256 GB RAM, and 1890 GB local storage, optimized for memory-heavy workloads.
tagline: A memory-optimized virtual machine featuring dedicated Intel Xeon processors, advanced matrix extensions, and high-capacity local storage for demanding enterprise workloads.
bullet_points: 32 dedicated vCPUs on an Intel Xeon 6973P-C processor running at 4.2 GHz
256.0 GB of system memory providing an 8:1 RAM-to-vCPU ratio
1890 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capability
Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction support for accelerated vector and matrix math
Suited for relational databases, Redis caching, and medium-scale LLM inference
